title: centaurea pallidior subsp. denudata
intro: Centaurea pallidior subsp. denudata, also known as Mountain cornflower, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Centaurea pallidior plant, which belongs to the daisy family. It is a flowering plant that is native to certain mountainous regions, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Centaurea pallidior subsp. denudata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
